heading: Humane methods
label: 1902 Humane methods
notes: Amendments1978—Par. (b). inserted “and handling in connection with such slaughtering” at end.Pub. L. 95–445

Effective Date of 1978 AmendmentAmendment by effective one year after , and nonapplicability during not to exceed additional 18 months in hardship cases, see sec. 7 of set out as a note under , Food and Drugs.Pub. L. 95–445Pub. L. 95–445section 603 of Title 21Oct. 10, 1978
amendments: Amendments1978—Par. (b). inserted “and handling in connection with such slaughtering” at end.Pub. L. 95–445

Effective Date of 1978 AmendmentAmendment by effective one year after , and nonapplicability during not to exceed additional 18 months in hardship cases, see sec. 7 of set out as a note under , Food and Drugs.Pub. L. 95–445Pub. L. 95–445section 603 of Title 21Oct. 10, 1978

children.label: a
children.text: in the case of cattle, calves, horses, mules, sheep, swine, and other livestock, all animals are rendered insensible to pain by a single blow or gunshot or an electrical, chemical or other means that is rapid and effective, before being shackled, hoisted, thrown, cast, or cut; or

children.label: b
children.text: by slaughtering in accordance with the ritual requirements of the Jewish faith or any other religious faith that prescribes a method of slaughter whereby the animal suffers loss of consciousness by anemia of the brain caused by the simultaneous and instantaneous severance of the carotid arteries with a sharp instrument and handling in connection with such slaughtering.
